name: guardrail-regime-ensemble
description: Meta-allocator that blends the four Track-2 strategy skills by market regime. For a classified regime, each skill contributes its example target portfolio with the weight listed below; the ensemble takes the weighted average of the per-skill target weights, renormalizes to <= 100, and holds the remainder as a USDT reserve. The Rust risk engine remains the sole execution gate; this config only proposes a blended target book.

risk_on.rationale: A broad, well-bid up-tape. Trend/breakout momentum and the general regime-routed alpha both deploy near full exposure (1.0x), so they lead. Funding carry adds a complementary tilt by leaning into cheaply-funded longs. Mean-reversion is suppressed because fading strength bleeds in a trending market.

risk_off.rationale: Falling-knife risk. Every skill de-risks to a large USDT reserve in risk_off, so the blend is dominated by capital preservation. The general regime-routed alpha (which rotates fully to USDT in risk_off) and funding carry (which de-risks regardless of funding) carry the most weight; the trend and reversion specialists are minimized because both bleed when initiating positions into a down-tape.

chop.rationale: A flat, range-bound tape. Mean-reversion is in its home regime (1.0x exposure) and earns the largest weight: fading oversold dips and trimming overbought rips is what pays in ranges. The general alpha provides a steady, diversified backbone. Trend/breakout and funding carry are cut hard because breakouts whipsaw and carry signals are noisy when there is no directional drift.

breakout.rationale: A strong, broad, confirmed advance. Trend/breakout momentum peaks here (1.1x exposure) and leads decisively. The general regime-routed alpha adds breadth across the strongest names. Funding carry contributes a small complementary tilt; mean-reversion is minimized because fading a confirmed breakout is the worst trade in this regime.
